expected_findings.rationale: response! crashes if network returns nil; should propagate as throws or return Optional
UserDefaults.string returns Optional<String>; force-unwrapping crashes on first launch when key is absent
Public method changed from User? to User — source-breaking change for callers. Per steering, source-breaking signature changes are blocker.

currentUserName() returns non-optional String but the underlying UserDefaults.string(forKey:) is Optional<String>. The signature lies to callers about whether nil is possible. Should be -> String? or take a default.

currentUserName() is a new public method introduced in this diff with no accompanying test. Per test_coverage.md, new public/open declarations without a test entry point in the diff are flagged at severity major.

must_not_flag: the let response binding (it is fine)
